candidates.name: Margaret A. Thornton
candidates.current_role: Chief Supply Chain Officer, HorizonTech Industries
notable_achievements:
My most significant achievement was the end-to-end digital transformation at HorizonTech Industries. I
designed and executed a 36-month programme that migrated 14 legal entities to SAP S/4HANA, deployed an
ML demand forecasting engine (reducing MAPE from 28% to 11%), and consolidated our 3PL base from 18 to 9
partners. Collectively, the programme delivered $120M in quantified savings, reduced OTIF gaps by 6.8
percentage points, and freed $42M in working capital from safety stock reduction. The programme was
completed on time, 6% under the approved $48M budget.
Delivered $120M+ in cumulative cost savings
Transformed complex, multi-region supply chains through data-driven strategy and digital platforms
Improved OTIF from 91% to 97.8%
Increased perfect order rate to 98.6% within 18 months
Deployed ML-based demand forecasting platform reducing forecast error (MAPE) from 28% to 11% and cutting safety stock by $42M
Redesigned global logistics network, consolidating 3PL base from 18 to 9 strategic partners and achieving $34M annual freight savings
End-to-end digital transformation at HorizonTech Industries, delivering $120M in quantified savings and reducing OTIF gaps by 6.8 percentage points
candidates.leadership_style: Clarity-driven, accountable, and invested in people, with a focus on measurable outcomes and constructive intervention
weaknesses: Potential over-reliance on technology for solutions
Need for more explicit conflict resolution strategies
Possible difficulty in delegating tasks and trusting team members to handle responsibilities

education.university: University of Chicago - Booth School of Business

motivation.why_best_suited:
My career has been built at the intersection of operational excellence and digital transformation — exactly what
this role demands. I have held C-suite supply chain responsibility for over six years, leading organisations of
comparable scale and complexity to VLG's. My experience delivering $120M+ in savings, executing an SAP
S/4HANA migration across 14 entities, and transforming a 24-country supply chain through ML forecasting and
IoT visibility positions me to accelerate VLG's stated transformation priorities from day one. I understand the
board-level visibility required of this role and have consistently earned the trust of CEOs and investors throughout my career.
motivation.biggest_achievement:
My most significant achievement was the end-to-end digital transformation at HorizonTech Industries. I
designed and executed a 36-month programme that migrated 14 legal entities to SAP S/4HANA, deployed an
ML demand forecasting engine (reducing MAPE from 28% to 11%), and consolidated our 3PL base from 18 to 9
partners. Collectively, the programme delivered $120M in quantified savings, reduced OTIF gaps by 6.8
percentage points, and freed $42M in working capital from safety stock reduction. The programme was
completed on time, 6% under the approved $48M budget.
motivation.leadership_approach:
I lead through clarity, accountability, and genuine investment in people. At the executive level, I establish
unambiguous strategic intent and hold my leadership team to measurable outcomes — but I stay close enough
to the work to intervene constructively when needed. I make decisions through structured frameworks and data,
but I never confuse analysis with action. On conflict: I surface it early, create psychological safety for dissent,
and resolve it at the lowest possible level. My teams describe my style as demanding but fair, and several of my
direct reports have been promoted into VP and C-suite roles at peer organisations.

candidates.name: David R. Kaplan
candidates.current_role: SVP, Supply Chain & Operations at Luminary Commerce
notable_achievements:
The build-out of Luminary Commerce's supply chain is my defining professional achievement. I inherited a
fragmented 12-person operation with no ERP integration and built it into a 95-person global function capable of
processing 80,000 daily orders at 99.5% accuracy. Key outcomes included $11.2M annual obsolescence
reduction, $9.4M freight savings, and 38% stockout improvement — all while scaling 5x in revenue. The
programme was completed in under 36 months, without disruption to customer experience.
Built supply chain function from 12-person team to 95-person global organisation at Luminary Commerce
Designed and deployed ML-driven inventory optimisation platform, reducing SKU-level stockouts by 38% and obsolescence write-offs by $11.2M annually
Established supplier ecosystem across APAC, negotiating average 14% unit cost reduction through volume commitments and localisation
Delivered $9.4M annual freight savings and 2.1-day improvement in average delivery time through 3PL RFP and transition to dual-partner model
Build-out of Luminary Commerce's supply chain, achieving $11.2M annual obsolescence reduction, $9.4M freight savings, and 38% stockout improvement while scaling 5x in revenue
candidates.leadership_style: Analytically driven with empathy, data-grounded decision-making, and investing in people development
weaknesses: Potential difficulty in adapting to a more established corporate setting
May face challenges in scaling beyond high-growth e-commerce and SaaS-adjacent businesses

motivation.why_best_suited:
I have spent the last five years scaling a supply chain from early-stage to nearly $1B GMV — a journey that
demanded every skill this role requires: strategic vision, technology enablement, talent building, and commercial
acumen. My experience building analytics-first supply chain organisations positions me to accelerate VLG's
digital roadmap. I have presented to boards, institutional investors, and audit committees, so I understand the
governance expectations of a CSCO at a company of VLG's scale. Unlike candidates from large corporates, I
bring an entrepreneurial velocity that is difficult to develop in a more established setting.
motivation.biggest_achievement:
The build-out of Luminary Commerce's supply chain is my defining professional achievement. I inherited a
fragmented 12-person operation with no ERP integration and built it into a 95-person global function capable of
processing 80,000 daily orders at 99.5% accuracy. Key outcomes included $11.2M annual obsolescence
reduction, $9.4M freight savings, and 38% stockout improvement — all while scaling 5x in revenue. The
programme was completed in under 36 months, without disruption to customer experience.
motivation.leadership_approach:
I lead analytically but with empathy. I believe every major decision should be data-grounded, but I also know
that execution is a human endeavour. I invest heavily in people development and have a strong track record of
retaining high performers in competitive talent markets. When conflict arises, I prefer to address it directly,
privately first, then collectively if needed — always anchoring the resolution to shared goals rather than
individual positions. My team would say I set an ambitious bar, explain the 'why' clearly, and give them the
autonomy to find the 'how'.

candidates.name: Priya Sundaram
candidates.current_role: VP, Global Supply Chain Strategy at Fortis Industrial Solutions
notable_achievements:
My proudest achievement is the procurement transformation at Fortis Industrial Solutions. Starting from a
fragmented, relationship-managed supplier base of 240 vendors, I built a category management capability,
deployed should-cost modelling, and rationalised to 110 strategic suppliers over 36 months — delivering $78M
in cumulative savings. Crucially, this was structural rather than transactional: savings recurred and grew
year-on-year. In parallel, I expanded dual-source coverage from 40% to 74% of critical spend, materially
reducing supply disruption risk.
Delivered $78M cumulative savings through procurement transformation at Fortis Industrial Solutions
Improved inventory turns from 6.1x to 8.9x over 3 years, releasing $31M working capital
Expanded dual-source coverage from 40% to 74% of critical spend, reducing supply disruption risk
Developed and implemented supply chain risk programme post-pandemic, reducing single-source risk events by 58%
candidates.leadership_style: Data-driven, structural solution-focused leader with high standards and deep respect for people
weaknesses: Potential over-reliance on data-driven decision making
May struggle with short-term, transactional solutions
Possibly limited experience with smaller, more agile organisations

education.university: Kellogg School of Management, Northwestern University

motivation.why_best_suited:
My 17 years span Fortune 100 industrials and global consumer goods — precisely the complex, multinational
environments VLG operates in. I have owned global supply chain strategy with full P&L; visibility, led teams of
85+, and delivered both hard-dollar savings ($78M+ cumulatively) and structural improvements in resilience and
service quality. My dual APICS certification and SAP S/4HANA leadership experience are directly applicable to
VLG's stated ERP modernisation agenda. I bring a balance of strategic depth and execution discipline that I
believe is rare and well-suited to this role.
motivation.biggest_achievement:
My proudest achievement is the procurement transformation at Fortis Industrial Solutions. Starting from a
fragmented, relationship-managed supplier base of 240 vendors, I built a category management capability,
deployed should-cost modelling, and rationalised to 110 strategic suppliers over 36 months — delivering $78M
in cumulative savings. Crucially, this was structural rather than transactional: savings recurred and grew
year-on-year. In parallel, I expanded dual-source coverage from 40% to 74% of critical spend, materially
reducing supply disruption risk.
motivation.leadership_approach:
I lead with high standards, deep respect for people, and a relentless focus on structural solutions. I don't tolerate
short-termism — I push my teams to fix root causes, not symptoms. My decision-making is disciplined: I use
data to frame the problem, scenarios to evaluate options, and clear criteria to choose. On conflict, I lean in
rather than deflect — I find that named conflict resolved early prevents larger misalignments later. I am
particularly proud of my track record developing talent: four of my direct reports have progressed to VP and
C-suite roles.

candidates.name: James T. Holloway
candidates.current_role: Senior Fulfilment Centre Manager at Quartz Commerce
notable_achievements:
My highest-impact achievement is the Manhattan Active WMS deployment across two Quartz Commerce
fulfilment centres. I owned the project end-to-end: requirements definition, vendor management, site training,
and go-live. The results were measurable: mis-pick rate fell from 1.8% to 0.28%, throughput improved 31%, and
dock-to-stock time halved. Critically, I did this while maintaining daily operations — we had zero SLA breaches
during the 9-month transition. The system has since become the group-wide standard, adopted by two
additional sites.
Exceeding SLAs while reducing cost-per-unit metrics
Reducing mis-pick rate from 1.8% to 0.28% and increasing throughput by 31% through Manhattan Active WMS deployment
Achieving zero OSHA recordable incidents for 24 consecutive months
Reducing labour cost per order from $2.84 to $2.11
Improving OTD from 93.1% to 97.6% without rate increase
candidates.leadership_style: Structured and presence-based with clarity of standards and investment in people's development
weaknesses: Potential overreliance on data-driven decision making
Limited experience with extremely large or complex logistics operations
Possible difficulty in adapting to radically different company cultures

motivation.why_best_suited:
I have spent 13 years in high-volume fulfilment environments that closely match VLG's Midwest network. I have
directly managed two fulfilment centres processing up to 28,000 orders daily, led WMS deployments, and
delivered measurable improvements in OTD, accuracy, cost, and safety. My Lean Six Sigma and APICS
credentials are active and applied — not academic. I understand the pressure of peak seasons, carrier SLA
disputes, and team development in a high-turnover environment. I am confident I can contribute materially from
week one.
motivation.biggest_achievement:
My highest-impact achievement is the Manhattan Active WMS deployment across two Quartz Commerce
fulfilment centres. I owned the project end-to-end: requirements definition, vendor management, site training,
and go-live. The results were measurable: mis-pick rate fell from 1.8% to 0.28%, throughput improved 31%, and
dock-to-stock time halved. Critically, I did this while maintaining daily operations — we had zero SLA breaches
during the 9-month transition. The system has since become the group-wide standard, adopted by two
additional sites.
motivation.leadership_approach:
My leadership style is structured and presence-based. I believe great operations require clarity of standards,
visible accountability, and genuine investment in people's development. I do daily floor walks, hold weekly 1:1s
with supervisors, and maintain open-door access for associates. When performance falls short, I address it
quickly and specifically — never publicly, always with a path forward. Conflict between teams is resolved
through data and shared KPIs: it's hard to argue with numbers. I have had zero involuntary supervisor turnover
in the last three years, which I consider my most underrated metric.

candidates.name: Rachel M. Torres
candidates.current_role: Operations Manager — Midwest Region at AxisFulfilment Inc.
notable_achievements:
Rebuilding the KPI reporting infrastructure at AxisFulfilment was my most impactful project. When I joined, KPI
data was produced manually from four separate systems, taking 6 hours per week and containing frequent
errors. I designed an integrated Power BI suite pulling live data from the WMS, TMS, and ERP, reducing
reporting time to 45 minutes and eliminating data discrepancies. The dashboards are now used daily by
operations supervisors and weekly by regional leadership. As a direct result, average OTD recovery time fell
from 3 days to same-day.
Reduced pick errors by 41% and improved slot utilisation by 19% through WMS upgrade project
Improved forecast adherence from 74% to 88% within 8 months
Developed supervisor training programme, reducing first-year supervisor attrition from 42% to 14%
Rebuilt KPI reporting infrastructure, reducing reporting time from 6 hours to 45 minutes and eliminating data discrepancies
Achieved order accuracy of 99.8% over 24 consecutive months
weaknesses: Possibly over-reliance on data-driven decision making

motivation.why_best_suited:
I offer a distinctive combination of operational depth and analytical capability that is uncommon at this level. I
have managed up to 35,000 daily orders across three sites, led WMS administration and improvement projects,
and built Power BI reporting infrastructure that my leadership team now depends on. My S&OP; experience —
improving forecast adherence from 74% to 88% — is directly relevant to VLG's cross-functional alignment goals.
I am rigorous with data but grounded in operational reality.
motivation.biggest_achievement:
Rebuilding the KPI reporting infrastructure at AxisFulfilment was my most impactful project. When I joined, KPI
data was produced manually from four separate systems, taking 6 hours per week and containing frequent
errors. I designed an integrated Power BI suite pulling live data from the WMS, TMS, and ERP, reducing
reporting time to 45 minutes and eliminating data discrepancies. The dashboards are now used daily by
operations supervisors and weekly by regional leadership. As a direct result, average OTD recovery time fell
from 3 days to same-day.
motivation.leadership_approach:
I lead by equipping people with information and trust. I believe in transparent performance data — everyone on
my team sees the same KPIs I do, and we review them together. Conflict is addressed through data first,
emotion second: most operational disputes dissolve when both sides see the same numbers. I invest in
supervisor development deliberately, which is why my attrition rate dropped from 42% to 14%. I make decisions
quickly when the data is clear, and consult broadly when it isn't.

candidates.name: Kevin A. Osei
candidates.current_role: Regional Operations Manager at ProChain Distribution Group
notable_achievements:
The Six Sigma DMAIC project targeting inbound receiving variance at ProChain is my standout achievement.
Receiving discrepancies were creating $620K in annual inventory writeoffs and causing downstream picking
errors. I led the full DMAIC cycle: mapped the process, identified root causes (primarily supplier labelling
inconsistency and manual receipt entry), designed control measures, and implemented automated ASN
matching in SAP EWM. The result was elimination of the writeoff, stock accuracy improvement from 96.1% to
99.4%, and a process Cpk of 1.42. Total annual benefit: approximately $890K including downstream picking
gains.
Eliminated $620K annual writeoffs and achieved process Cpk of 1.42 through Six Sigma DMAIC initiative
Improved stock accuracy from 96.1% to 99.4%
Achieved $1.1M annual freight savings through 3PL contract restructuring
Improved on-time delivery to 96.8%
Reduced damage claims by 44% through enhanced packing standards
Increased team productivity by 19% through visual management boards and daily stand-ups
candidates.leadership_style: Leading by standards, structure, and presence with a focus on process definition and training
weaknesses: Potential over-reliance on technical tools and certifications

motivation.why_best_suited:
My background in high-volume industrial distribution — managing safety-critical, multi-site operations under
strict SLAs — directly maps to VLG's Midwest fulfilment remit. I am a credentialled Six Sigma Black Belt with
real-world project ROI, an SAP EWM certified administrator, and a manager who has delivered measurable cost
and quality improvements across every role. I bring a structured problem-solving mindset combined with strong
floor leadership.
motivation.biggest_achievement:
The Six Sigma DMAIC project targeting inbound receiving variance at ProChain is my standout achievement.
Receiving discrepancies were creating $620K in annual inventory writeoffs and causing downstream picking
errors. I led the full DMAIC cycle: mapped the process, identified root causes (primarily supplier labelling
inconsistency and manual receipt entry), designed control measures, and implemented automated ASN
matching in SAP EWM. The result was elimination of the writeoff, stock accuracy improvement from 96.1% to
99.4%, and a process Cpk of 1.42. Total annual benefit: approximately $890K including downstream picking
gains.
motivation.leadership_approach:
I lead by standards, structure, and presence. I believe that most operational failures are process failures, not
people failures — so I invest heavily in defining and training the right process before holding people
accountable. My team knows exactly what 'good' looks like, sees their own KPIs daily, and has the tools to
self-correct. When performance falls short, I diagnose before I react. On conflict: I bring it into the open with
data, keep emotions out of it, and close with a clear agreed action.
